name: shap
tagline: Reference guide for using SHAP to explain ML model predictions: explainer selection, plots, workflows, and troubleshooting.
verdict: A well-organized, purely educational SHAP reference with no security concerns — no network calls, secrets, or destructive operations. Weak spots are process discipline: no stated exclusions/negative triggers, some workflow steps stay hand-wavy, no fixed output contract, an unpinned install of the well-known 'shap' package, and mlflow/joblib used in examples.

highlights.summary: Line 542: `uv pip install shap` — well-known PyPI package but installed unpinned (also `-U` for latest at line 552 area).
No 'NOT for' / out-of-scope / pointer-to-other-skill text anywhere despite a very broad 'works with any model' claim.

Several Core Workflow steps are hand-wavy in SKILL.md itself, e.g. 'Validate feature relationships make sense' and 'Implement mitigation strategies if bias found', with no stated criteria (deferred to
